checks.finding: T-001 through T-005 appropriately use high risk and TDD for integrity-boundary and mandatory-gate changes whose silent failure could permit invalid persisted state or releases; T-006 appropriately uses medium risk and acceptance-first for reversible release metadata.

In tasks.md, every high-risk task T-001 through T-005 plans Red→Green evidence, an independent-review verdict, and spec_revision, but none requires environment provenance, and none requires evidence that the mandatory requirement-traceability check passed. The risk-gate matrix explicitly requires provenance (spec revision plus environment) and requirement-traceability evidence for high-risk tasks. A downstream quality contract can therefore lack mandatory high-tier evidence, so this is blocking at the check's required Critical severity.

Each task is bounded to one cohesive deliverable with specific files, scope, completion evidence, and exclusions; the broader portable-validator and integration work is separated into T-003, T-004, and T-005.

The decomposition covers malformed and unreadable inputs, stale and forged provenance, traversal and symlink escape, predecessor inversions, lifecycle bypasses, legacy overbreadth, lite isolation, LF/CRLF parity, CI ordering, and release-version skew.

High-risk behavioral tasks T-001 through T-005 require TDD with Red→Green evidence and targeted unit, parity, integration, or regression suites; medium-risk metadata task T-006 uses acceptance-first version-consistency and install/release validation.
Every task specifies a bounded rollback unit and an inspectable exercise or validation of the rollback, including isolated fixtures where repository state would otherwise be affected.

Task scopes separate transition repair, registry migration, validator implementation, repository/CI wiring, gate wiring, and release metadata. Necessary shared-file edits are sequenced and concern-distinct.

Overlapping files are dependency-ordered: T-005 follows T-003, which follows T-001/T-002, and T-006 follows T-004/T-005. The validator correctly depends on both the precheck repair and registry contract.

T-001 preserves a complete diagnostic path from the observed Pending-to-Passed transition failure, through canonical hash/path mismatch diagnosis, to a narrow fix, failing-before/passing-after evidence, regression fixtures, and rollback reproduction.
